en.extract:
The Timor friarbird or plain friarbird (Philemon inornatus) is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is found on Timor island.


Habitat
It is found on Timor island, where it plays an important cultural role. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical dry forests; it tends to be most present near roads, particularly flat low-traffic roads near rivers and mangroves. It is a species generalist.


Threats
It is threatened by human-caused habitat loss—caused by logging, forest fires, and illegitimate grazing—and poaching. Over the past decade, its population has decreased significantly.


Diet
It feeds primarily on nectar, and also eats insects and fruit.


Social behavior
Timor friarbirds live in pairs or small groups.


References

The '''Timor friarbird''' or '''plain friarbird''' ('''''Philemon inornatus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]]. It is found on [[Timor]] island.

== Habitat ==
It is found on [[Timor]] island, where it plays an important cultural role.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Paga |first1=Blasius |last2=PUDYATMOKO |first2=SATYAWAN |last3=FAIDA |first3=LIES RAHAYU WIJAYANTI |last4=YUDA |first4=IGN. PRAMANA |last5=SULAKSONO |first5=NURPANA |date=2021-04-28 |title=Characteristics of vegetation as determinant of Timor Friarbird (Philemon inornatus) distribution in Bipolo Lanscape [sic] of West Timor Island, Indonesia |journal=Biodiversitas Journal of Biological Diversity |volume=22 |issue=5 |doi=10.13057/biodiv/d220521 |issn=2085-4722|doi-access=free }}</ref> Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical dry [[forest]]s; it tends to be most present near roads, particularly flat low-traffic roads near rivers and mangroves.<ref name=":1">{{Cite journal |last1=Paga |first1=Blasius |last2=PUDYATMOKO |first2=SATYAWAN |last3=WIJAYANTI |first3=LIES RAHAYU |last4=YUDA |first4=PRAMANA |last5=SULAKSONO |first5=NURPANA |date=2022-01-26 |title=The habitat suitability of Timor friarbird (Philemon inornatus) in Western Timor Island, Indonesia |journal=Biodiversitas Journal of Biological Diversity |volume=23 |issue=2 |doi=10.13057/biodiv/d230215 |issn=2085-4722|doi-access=free }}</ref> It is a species generalist.<ref name=":0" />

== Threats ==
It is threatened by human-caused habitat loss—caused by logging, forest fires, and illegitimate grazing<ref name=":1" />—and poaching.<ref name=":0" /> Over the past decade, its population has decreased significantly.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Paga |first1=B |last2=Pudyatmoko |first2=S |last3=Faida |first3=L R W |last4=Yuda |first4=P |date=2022-12-01 |title=Recent population size of Timor Friarbird and resources utilization at three landscapes in West Timor Island, Indonesia |journal=IOP Conference Series: Earth and Environmental Science |volume=1107 |issue=1 |article-number=012122 |doi=10.1088/1755-1315/1107/1/012122 |issn=1755-1307|doi-access=free |bibcode=2022E&ES.1107a2122P }}</ref>

== Diet ==
It feeds primarily on nectar, and also eats insects and fruit.<ref name=":0" />

== Social behavior ==
Timor friarbirds live in pairs or small groups.<ref name=":0" />
